passionate.en: feeling or showing strong enthusiasm for something

ex.en: She is passionate about music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 음악에 열정적이다.

passionate.en: showing very strong feelings, especially of love or anger

ex.en: They shared a passionate kiss at the airport.
ex.ko: 그들은 공항에서 격정적인 키스를 나눴다.

ex.en: She spoke passionately about human rights.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인권에 대해 열정적으로 이야기했다.

pastor.en: a religious leader in charge of a church, especially in Protestant Christianity

ex.en: The pastor gave an inspiring sermon on Sunday.
ex.ko: 목사님은 일요일에 감동적인 설교를 했다.

pastoral.en: relating to a peaceful and idealized version of country life

ex.en: The village has a pastoral charm with green fields and grazing sheep.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 초록 들판과 풀을 뜯는 양들이 있는 목가적인 매력을 지니고 있다.

pastoral.en: relating to the spiritual duties and care given by a pastor or priest

ex.en: The bishop offered pastoral care to the sick.
ex.ko: 주교는 병자들에게 목회적 돌봄을 제공했다.

patch.en: a small piece of material used to cover a hole or repair something

ex.en: She sewed a patch onto the torn jeans.
ex.ko: 그녀는 찢어진 청바지에 헝겊 조각을 꿰맸다.

patch.en: mend something by covering a hole or damaged area

ex.en: He patched the hole in the roof.
ex.ko: 그는 지붕에 난 구멍을 때웠다.

patch.en: a small area of land, especially one used for growing something

ex.en: They grow vegetables in a small patch behind the house.
ex.ko: 그들은 집 뒤편 작은 땅에서 채소를 기른다.

patch.en: a small piece of software code used to fix or update a computer program

ex.en: The company released a patch to fix the security bug.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 보안 결함을 고치는 패치를 배포했다.

patch.en: a period of time, especially a difficult one

ex.en: The team is going through a rough patch this season.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 이번 시즌 힘든 시기를 겪고 있다.

patiently.en: in a patient way, without becoming annoyed while waiting

ex.en: She waited patiently for the bus.
ex.ko: 그녀는 참을성 있게 버스를 기다렸다.

patriot.en: a person who loves and strongly supports their country

ex.en: He fought for his country like a true patriot.
ex.ko: 그는 진정한 애국자처럼 조국을 위해 싸웠다.

patriotic.en: showing love and support for your country

ex.en: They sang patriotic songs on Independence Day.
ex.ko: 그들은 독립기념일에 애국적인 노래를 불렀다.

patriotism.en: love and devotion to one's country

ex.en: The soldiers showed great patriotism during the war.
ex.ko: 군인들은 전쟁 중에 큰 애국심을 보여주었다.

patron.en: a regular customer of a shop, restaurant, or other business

ex.en: The restaurant thanked its loyal patrons for years of support.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 오랜 세월 지지해준 단골손님들에게 감사를 전했다.

patron.en: a person who gives financial or other support to a person, cause, or organization

ex.en: The museum relies on wealthy patrons for funding.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 자금을 위해 부유한 후원자들에게 의존한다.

pave.en: cover a road or path with a hard surface such as stone or concrete

ex.en: Workers paved the new road last month.
ex.ko: 인부들은 지난달 새 도로를 포장했다.

pave.en: (pave the way) make it possible or easier for something to happen later

ex.en: Her research paved the way for new treatments.
ex.ko: 그녀의 연구는 새로운 치료법을 위한 길을 닦았다.

pavement.en: a paved path beside a road for people to walk on

ex.en: Children were riding bikes on the pavement.
ex.ko: 아이들이 인도에서 자전거를 타고 있었다.

pavement.en: the hard surface of a road or path

ex.en: The pavement was cracked after the earthquake.
ex.ko: 지진 이후 도로 표면이 갈라졌다.

paw.en: the foot of an animal that has claws, such as a dog or cat

ex.en: The dog scratched at the door with its paw.
ex.ko: 그 개는 발로 문을 긁었다.

paw.en: touch or scrape something with a paw, or clumsily with the hands

ex.en: The cat pawed at the toy on the floor.
ex.ko: 고양이가 바닥에 있는 장난감을 발로 건드렸다.

pawnbroker.cue: 돈을 빌려주고 물건을 담보로 잡는 사람
pawnbroker.en: a person who lends money in exchange for personal items left as security

ex.en: He took his watch to the pawnbroker for a quick loan.
ex.ko: 그는 급전을 빌리려고 시계를 전당포 주인에게 맡겼다.

pawnshop.en: a shop where you can borrow money by leaving an item as security

ex.en: She sold her ring to a pawnshop for cash.
ex.ko: 그녀는 현금을 마련하려고 전당포에 반지를 팔았다.

payment.en: an amount of money that is paid, or the act of paying it

ex.en: The final payment is due next week.
ex.ko: 최종 결제는 다음 주까지다.

peacefully.en: in a calm and peaceful way, without violence or disturbance

ex.en: The old man died peacefully in his sleep.
ex.ko: 그 노인은 잠결에 평온하게 세상을 떠났다.

peach.en: a soft, round, juicy fruit with a fuzzy skin

ex.en: I bought some fresh peaches at the market.
ex.ko: 나는 시장에서 신선한 복숭아를 좀 샀다.

ex.en: She painted the wall a soft peach color.
ex.ko: 그녀는 벽을 부드러운 복숭아색으로 칠했다.

peak.en: the pointed top of a mountain

ex.en: They reached the peak of the mountain at sunset.
ex.ko: 그들은 해질녘에 산 정상에 도달했다.

peak.en: the highest point or greatest amount of something

ex.en: Traffic reaches its peak around 6 p.m.
ex.ko: 교통량은 오후 6시쯤 최고조에 달한다.

peak.en: reach the highest point or greatest value

ex.en: Sales peaked in December.
ex.ko: 매출은 12월에 정점에 달했다.

pearl.en: a small, hard, shiny white ball formed inside the shell of an oyster, used in jewelry

ex.en: She wore a necklace of pearls.
ex.ko: 그녀는 진주 목걸이를 착용했다.

pebble.en: a small smooth stone, especially one found on a beach or riverbed

ex.en: The children threw pebbles into the lake.
ex.ko: 아이들이 호수에 조약돌을 던졌다.

pedal.en: a flat part that you press with your foot to make a machine, such as a bicycle or car, work

ex.en: He pressed the brake pedal suddenly.
ex.ko: 그는 갑자기 브레이크 페달을 밟았다.

pedal.en: press the pedals of a bicycle or machine with your feet

ex.en: She pedaled hard up the hill.
ex.ko: 그녀는 언덕을 오르며 힘껏 페달을 밟았다.

pedantic.en: too concerned with small details or formal rules, especially in a way that seems boring

ex.en: His pedantic corrections annoyed everyone in the meeting.
ex.ko: 그의 현학적인 지적은 회의에 있던 모두를 짜증나게 했다.

pedestrian.en: a person who is walking, especially in a street or town

ex.en: Drivers must yield to pedestrians at the crosswalk.
ex.ko: 운전자는 횡단보도에서 보행자에게 양보해야 한다.

ex.en: The movie had a pedestrian plot with no surprises.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 놀랄 것 없는 평범한 줄거리였다.

ex.en: The dog needs to go outside to pee.
ex.ko: 그 개는 밖에 나가서 소변을 봐야 한다.

pee.en: (informal) urine, or the act of urinating

ex.en: I need to take a pee before we leave.
ex.ko: 출발하기 전에 소변을 봐야겠다.

peel.en: remove the skin or outer covering from a fruit or vegetable

ex.en: She peeled an apple for her son.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아들을 위해 사과 껍질을 벗겼다.

peel.en: the skin or outer covering of a fruit or vegetable

ex.en: Don't throw away the potato peel.
ex.ko: 감자 껍질을 버리지 마.

peel.en: (of skin, paint, etc.) come off in thin pieces

ex.en: The paint on the wall is starting to peel.
ex.ko: 벽에 칠한 페인트가 벗겨지기 시작하고 있다.

peep.en: look quickly and secretly at something

ex.en: He peeped through the curtains to see who was outside.
ex.ko: 그는 밖에 누가 있는지 커튼 틈으로 엿보았다.

peep.en: a quick secret look at something

ex.en: She took a peep at the surprise gift.
ex.ko: 그녀는 깜짝 선물을 살짝 들여다보았다.

peer.en: a person who is the same age, status, or ability as you

ex.en: Teenagers often care what their peers think.
ex.ko: 십대들은 종종 또래들이 어떻게 생각하는지 신경 쓴다.

peer.en: look closely or carefully, especially because something is hard to see

ex.en: She peered into the dark room.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어두운 방 안을 유심히 들여다보았다.

peg.en: a small hook or clip used for hanging things such as coats or clothes

ex.en: Hang your coat on the peg by the door.
ex.ko: 문 옆 걸이못에 코트를 걸어 둬.

peg.en: fix the value of a currency or price at a specific rate

ex.en: The country pegged its currency to the US dollar.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 자국 통화를 미국 달러에 연동시켰다.

penalize.en: punish someone or put them at a disadvantage for breaking a rule

ex.en: Students will be penalized for late submissions.
ex.ko: 학생들은 늦게 제출하면 불이익을 받을 것이다.

penalty.en: a punishment for breaking a law, rule, or contract

ex.en: There is a penalty for parking illegally.
ex.ko: 불법 주차에는 벌금이 부과된다.

penalty.en: a disadvantage given to a team or player for breaking the rules of a game

ex.en: The referee awarded a penalty kick after the foul.
ex.ko: 심판은 반칙 후 페널티킥을 선언했다.

penetrate.en: go into or through something, often overcoming resistance

ex.en: The bullet penetrated the wall.
ex.ko: 총알이 벽을 관통했다.

penetrate.en: succeed in entering or gaining influence in a market, organization, or group

ex.en: The company is trying to penetrate the Asian market.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 아시아 시장에 침투하려 하고 있다.

penguin.en: a black-and-white sea bird that cannot fly and lives mainly in cold Antarctic regions

ex.en: Penguins waddled across the ice.
ex.ko: 펭귄들이 뒤뚱거리며 얼음 위를 걸었다.

penicillin.en: an antibiotic medicine used to treat bacterial infections

ex.en: The doctor prescribed penicillin for the infection.
ex.ko: 의사는 감염 치료를 위해 페니실린을 처방했다.

pension.en: a regular payment made to someone, usually after they retire from work

ex.en: She receives a pension from her former employer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 예전 직장에서 연금을 받는다.

pentagon.en: a flat shape with five straight sides

ex.en: A stop sign is shaped differently from a pentagon.
ex.ko: 정지 표지판은 오각형과는 다른 모양이다.

pentagon.en: the headquarters building of the US Department of Defense, or the US military leadership itself

ex.en: The Pentagon announced new defense plans.
ex.ko: 미국 국방부는 새로운 방위 계획을 발표했다.

perceive.en: become aware of something through the senses or the mind

ex.en: She perceived a change in his tone of voice.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 말투에서 변화를 알아차렸다.

perceive.en: think of someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: The plan was perceived as too risky.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 너무 위험한 것으로 여겨졌다.

percentage.en: an amount or proportion of something, expressed as part of a hundred

ex.en: What percentage of students passed the exam?
ex.ko: 학생들 중 몇 퍼센트가 시험에 합격했나요?

perception.en: the way someone understands or thinks about something

ex.en: Public perception of the issue has changed.
ex.ko: 그 사안에 대한 대중의 인식이 바뀌었다.

perception.en: the ability to notice or understand something through the senses

ex.en: Alcohol can affect your perception of time.
ex.ko: 술은 시간에 대한 지각에 영향을 줄 수 있다.

permanence.en: the quality of lasting or existing forever, or for a long time

ex.en: Nothing in life has true permanence.
ex.ko: 삶에서 진정으로 영속적인 것은 없다.

permeate.en: spread through and be present in every part of something

ex.en: The smell of coffee permeated the whole house.
ex.ko: 커피 향이 집 안 전체에 스며들었다.

perseverance.en: continued effort to do or achieve something despite difficulties

ex.en: Success in this field requires patience and perseverance.
ex.ko: 이 분야에서 성공하려면 인내와 끈기가 필요하다.

persist.en: continue to exist or happen, especially for longer than expected

ex.en: If the pain persists, see a doctor.
ex.ko: 통증이 계속되면 의사에게 진료를 받아라.

persist.en: continue doing something despite difficulty or opposition

ex.en: She persisted with her studies despite the setbacks.
ex.ko: 그녀는 좌절에도 불구하고 공부를 계속 밀고 나갔다.

persistence.en: the quality of continuing firmly despite difficulty or opposition

ex.en: Her persistence finally paid off when she got the job.
ex.ko: 그녀의 끈기는 결국 취업이라는 결실을 맺었다.

persistent.en: continuing to happen or exist for a long time, without stopping

ex.en: He has a persistent cough that won't go away.
ex.ko: 그는 낫지 않는 지속적인 기침을 앓고 있다.

persistent.en: (of a person) continuing firmly despite difficulty or opposition

ex.en: She was persistent in asking for a refund.
ex.ko: 그녀는 환불을 요구하며 끈질기게 매달렸다.

personification.en: a person who is a perfect example of a particular quality

ex.en: He is the personification of patience.
ex.ko: 그는 인내심의 화신이다.

personification.en: (in literature) giving human qualities to an object or idea

ex.en: The poem uses personification to describe the wind as a person.
ex.ko: 그 시는 바람을 사람처럼 묘사하는 의인화를 사용한다.

personify.en: be a perfect example of a particular quality

ex.en: She personifies elegance and grace.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우아함과 기품 그 자체다.

personify.en: represent an object, quality, or idea as having human characteristics

ex.en: The novel personifies death as an old man.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 죽음을 노인으로 의인화한다.

personnel.en: the people who work for an organization or business, considered as a group

ex.en: The company is hiring additional personnel for the new project.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새 프로젝트를 위해 추가 인원을 채용하고 있다.

personnel.en: the department in a company responsible for hiring and managing employees

ex.en: Contact personnel for questions about your benefits.
ex.ko: 복지 혜택에 관한 질문은 인사부에 문의하세요.

perspective.en: a particular way of thinking about or looking at something

ex.en: Try to see the problem from her perspective.
ex.ko: 그녀의 관점에서 그 문제를 보려고 노력해봐.

perspective.en: the technique of drawing objects so they appear to have depth and distance

ex.en: The artist used perspective to make the road look long.
ex.ko: 그 화가는 원근법을 사용해 길이 길어 보이게 그렸다.

perspiration.en: the salty liquid that comes out through your skin, especially when you are hot or exercising

ex.en: His shirt was soaked with perspiration after the run.
ex.ko: 달리기를 마친 후 그의 셔츠는 땀에 흠뻑 젖었다.

persuasion.en: the act of persuading someone to do or believe something

ex.en: It took a lot of persuasion to get him to agree.
ex.ko: 그를 동의시키는 데는 많은 설득이 필요했다.

pessimist.en: a person who always expects the worst possible outcome

ex.en: He's such a pessimist that he never expects good news.
ex.ko: 그는 워낙 비관주의자라 좋은 소식을 기대하는 법이 없다.

pessimistic.en: expecting the worst possible outcome in a situation

ex.en: She feels pessimistic about the company's future.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회사의 미래에 대해 비관적으로 느낀다.

petrified.en: so frightened that you cannot move or think

ex.en: She was petrified when she saw the spider.
ex.ko: 그녀는 거미를 보고 겁에 질렸다.

petrified.en: (of wood or another organic substance) turned into stone over a very long time

ex.en: They found a piece of petrified wood in the desert.
ex.ko: 그들은 사막에서 화석화된 나무 조각을 발견했다.

ex.en: Let's not argue over petty details.
ex.ko: 사소한 것들로 다투지 말자.

petty.en: showing that you care too much about small, unimportant things, especially in an unkind way

ex.en: It was petty of him to complain about who got the bigger slice.
ex.ko: 누가 더 큰 조각을 받았는지 불평하다니 그는 참 옹졸했다.

pH.en: a scale used to measure how acidic or alkaline a substance is

ex.en: The pH of pure water is 7.
ex.ko: 순수한 물의 pH는 7이다.

pharmacist.en: a person who is trained to prepare and sell medicines

ex.en: Ask the pharmacist if you have questions about the medication.
ex.ko: 약에 대해 궁금한 점이 있으면 약사에게 물어보세요.

phase.en: a stage in a process of change or development

ex.en: The project is entering its final phase.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 마지막 단계에 접어들고 있다.

phase.en: one of the recurring shapes of the visible part of the moon

ex.en: The phases of the moon include the full moon and new moon.
ex.ko: 달의 위상에는 보름달과 초승달이 포함된다.

philosophical.en: relating to philosophy or the study of ideas about knowledge and existence

ex.en: They had a philosophical discussion about the meaning of life.
ex.ko: 그들은 삶의 의미에 대해 철학적인 대화를 나눴다.

philosophical.en: calmly accepting a difficult situation rather than being upset by it

ex.en: He was philosophical about losing his job.
ex.ko: 그는 실직에 대해 담담하게 받아들였다.

physician.en: a medical doctor, especially one who treats illnesses rather than performing surgery

ex.en: You should see a physician about that cough.
ex.ko: 그 기침 때문에 의사에게 가봐야 해.

pi.en: the mathematical constant representing the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, approximately 3.14

ex.en: The value of pi is roughly 3.14159.
ex.ko: 파이의 값은 대략 3.14159이다.

pickle.en: a vegetable, especially a cucumber, preserved in vinegar or salty water

ex.en: He added a pickle to his sandwich.
ex.ko: 그는 샌드위치에 피클을 곁들였다.

pickle.en: (informal, in a pickle) a difficult or awkward situation

ex.en: We're in a real pickle if the car won't start.
ex.ko: 차 시동이 안 걸리면 우리는 정말 곤경에 처하게 된다.

picky.en: choosing or accepting only what you like; hard to please

ex.en: My little brother is a picky eater.
ex.ko: 내 남동생은 음식을 가리는 까다로운 아이다.

picturesque.en: (of a place) attractive in a charming or quaint way, like in a painting

ex.en: The village is famous for its picturesque streets.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 그림 같은 거리로 유명하다.

pillowcase.en: a cloth cover that fits over a pillow

ex.en: She changed the pillowcase before her guests arrived.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손님이 오기 전에 베갯잇을 갈았다.

pine.en: an evergreen tree with thin, sharp leaves called needles

ex.en: The forest was full of tall pine trees.
ex.ko: 그 숲은 키 큰 소나무로 가득했다.

pine.en: (pine for) feel a strong, sad longing for someone or something

ex.en: She pined for her hometown after moving abroad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 해외로 이주한 후 고향을 애타게 그리워했다.

pint.en: a unit for measuring liquid, often used for beer or milk (about 0.47–0.57 liters)

ex.en: He ordered a pint of beer at the pub.
ex.ko: 그는 술집에서 맥주 한 파인트를 주문했다.

piracy.en: the crime of attacking and robbing ships at sea

ex.en: Piracy has increased near this coastline in recent years.
ex.ko: 최근 이 해안 근처에서 해적 행위가 늘었다.

piracy.en: the illegal copying or use of someone else's creative work, such as music or software

ex.en: The film industry loses billions of dollars to piracy.
ex.ko: 영화 산업은 불법 복제로 수십억 달러의 손실을 본다.

pirate.en: a person who attacks and robs ships at sea

ex.en: The pirates attacked the merchant ship.
ex.ko: 해적들이 상선을 공격했다.

pirate.en: illegally copy or use someone's creative work without permission

ex.en: It's illegal to pirate movies and music online.
ex.ko: 온라인에서 영화와 음악을 불법 복제하는 것은 불법이다.

pitch.en: how high or low a sound is

ex.en: She sang the note at the wrong pitch.
ex.ko: 그녀는 틀린 음정으로 그 음을 불렀다.

pitch.en: throw a ball, especially in baseball or cricket

ex.en: He pitched the ball straight to the batter.
ex.ko: 그는 타자에게 공을 곧장 던졌다.

pitch.en: a marked area of ground used for playing sports such as soccer

ex.en: The players ran onto the pitch.
ex.ko: 선수들이 경기장으로 뛰어 들어왔다.

pitch.en: a talk in which someone tries to persuade others to buy something or support an idea

ex.en: The startup gave a sales pitch to investors.
ex.ko: 그 스타트업은 투자자들에게 홍보 발표를 했다.

pitch.en: fall or move forward suddenly and steeply

ex.en: The boat pitched forward in the storm.
ex.ko: 폭풍 속에서 배가 앞으로 심하게 기울었다.

plague.en: a deadly disease that spreads quickly to a large number of people

ex.en: The plague killed millions of people in medieval Europe.
ex.ko: 역병은 중세 유럽에서 수백만 명의 목숨을 앗아갔다.

plague.en: cause someone continual trouble or suffering

ex.en: He has been plagued by health problems for years.
ex.ko: 그는 수년간 건강 문제에 시달려 왔다.

plague.en: a large number of insects or pests that cause damage

ex.en: A plague of locusts destroyed the crops.
ex.ko: 메뚜기 떼가 농작물을 초토화시켰다.

planning.en: the process of deciding in detail how to do something in the future

ex.en: Good planning is essential for a successful event.
ex.ko: 성공적인 행사를 위해서는 좋은 기획이 필수적이다.

playmate.en: a friend, especially a child, that you play with

ex.en: The two boys have been playmates since kindergarten.
ex.ko: 그 두 소년은 유치원 때부터 놀이 친구였다.

ex.en: Shakespeare is the most famous English playwright.
ex.ko: 셰익스피어는 가장 유명한 영국 극작가다.

plead.en: ask for something in a very serious and emotional way

ex.en: She pleaded with the judge for mercy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 판사에게 선처를 간청했다.

plead.en: formally state in court whether you are guilty or not guilty of a crime

ex.en: The defendant pleaded not guilty to the charges.
ex.ko: 피고인은 혐의에 대해 무죄를 주장했다.

plot.en: the main events of a story in a book, film, or play

ex.en: The plot of the novel is full of twists.
ex.ko: 그 소설의 줄거리는 반전으로 가득하다.

plot.en: a secret plan made by a group of people to do something illegal or harmful

ex.en: The police uncovered a plot to rob the bank.
ex.ko: 경찰은 은행을 털려는 음모를 적발했다.

plot.en: plan secretly with others to do something illegal or harmful

ex.en: They plotted to overthrow the government.
ex.ko: 그들은 정부를 전복하려는 음모를 꾸몄다.

plot.en: a small piece of land, especially one marked out for a purpose

ex.en: They bought a plot of land to build a house.
ex.ko: 그들은 집을 지으려고 작은 땅을 샀다.

plot.en: mark points or a line on a graph or chart to show data

ex.en: She plotted the results on a graph.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결과를 그래프에 표시했다.

pluck.en: pull feathers or hairs out of something

ex.en: She plucked the chicken before cooking it.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요리하기 전에 닭의 털을 뽑았다.

pluck.en: pull and release the strings of a musical instrument with your fingers

ex.en: He gently plucked the guitar strings.
ex.ko: 그는 기타 줄을 부드럽게 뜯었다.

pluck.en: (informal) courage and determination in a difficult situation

ex.en: It took a lot of pluck to speak up in front of everyone.
ex.ko: 모두 앞에서 목소리를 내는 데는 상당한 배짱이 필요했다.

plum.en: a small, round, juicy fruit with smooth purple or red skin

ex.en: She picked ripe plums from the tree.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나무에서 잘 익은 자두를 땄다.

plum.en: (of a job or position) highly desirable

ex.en: He landed a plum job at a top law firm.
ex.ko: 그는 최고 로펌에서 탐나는 자리를 얻었다.

plumber.en: a person whose job is to install and repair water pipes and fixtures

ex.en: We called a plumber to fix the leaking sink.
ex.ko: 우리는 물이 새는 싱크대를 고치려고 배관공을 불렀다.

pneumonia.en: a serious illness in which the lungs become infected and inflamed

ex.en: He was hospitalized with pneumonia.
ex.ko: 그는 폐렴으로 입원했다.

point of view.en: a particular way of considering or judging a situation

ex.en: From my point of view, the plan makes sense.
ex.ko: 내 관점에서는 그 계획이 타당해 보인다.

ex.en: It's pointless to argue about something we can't change.
ex.ko: 우리가 바꿀 수 없는 일로 논쟁하는 것은 무의미하다.

polar bear.en: a large white bear that lives in the Arctic

ex.en: Polar bears hunt seals on the ice.
ex.ko: 북극곰은 얼음 위에서 물개를 사냥한다.

polio.en: a serious disease caused by a virus that can lead to paralysis, especially in children

ex.en: The polio vaccine has saved millions of lives.
ex.ko: 소아마비 백신은 수백만 명의 목숨을 구했다.

polish.en: rub something to make it smooth and shiny

ex.en: He polished his shoes before the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접 전에 구두를 광이 나게 닦았다.

polish.en: a smooth, shiny surface, or a substance used to produce it

ex.en: The table has a beautiful polish.
ex.ko: 그 탁자는 아름다운 광택을 지니고 있다.

polish.en: improve something by making small changes and corrections

ex.en: She polished her essay before submitting it.
ex.ko: 그녀는 제출하기 전에 에세이를 다듬었다.

politeness.en: polite behavior that shows respect for others

ex.en: He greeted everyone with great politeness.
ex.ko: 그는 모든 사람을 매우 공손하게 맞이했다.

politically.en: in a way that relates to government or politics

ex.en: The country is politically unstable right now.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 지금 정치적으로 불안정하다.

polity.en: the political organization or form of government of a society or state

ex.en: Ancient Athens had a democratic polity.
ex.ko: 고대 아테네는 민주적인 정치 체제를 갖고 있었다.

polo.en: a game played on horseback in which players use long mallets to hit a ball

ex.en: The prince enjoys playing polo on weekends.
ex.ko: 왕자는 주말마다 폴로 경기를 즐긴다.

polo.en: (informal, short for polo shirt) a casual shirt with a collar and short sleeves

ex.en: He wore a blue polo and khaki pants.
ex.ko: 그는 파란 폴로셔츠와 카키색 바지를 입었다.

ponder.en: think carefully about something for a period of time

ex.en: She pondered the question before answering.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대답하기 전에 그 질문을 곰곰이 생각했다.

popularity.en: the quality of being liked or admired by many people

ex.en: The band's popularity grew rapidly after the concert.
ex.ko: 그 밴드의 인기는 콘서트 이후 빠르게 높아졌다.

popularize.en: make something widely known, understood, or liked by ordinary people

ex.en: The internet helped popularize online shopping.
ex.ko: 인터넷은 온라인 쇼핑을 대중화하는 데 기여했다.

porch.cue: 집 현관 앞 지붕 있는 공간
porch.en: a covered structure at the entrance of a house

ex.en: They sat on the porch and watched the sunset.
ex.ko: 그들은 현관에 앉아 노을을 바라보았다.

portion.en: a part of something larger, especially a share given to someone

ex.en: Each employee received a portion of the profits.
ex.ko: 각 직원은 이익의 일부를 받았다.

portion.en: an amount of food served to one person

ex.en: The restaurant serves generous portions.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 넉넉한 양의 1인분을 제공한다.

pose.en: hold a particular position of the body, especially for a photograph or painting

ex.en: They posed for a photo in front of the museum.
ex.ko: 그들은 박물관 앞에서 사진을 찍기 위해 포즈를 취했다.

pose.en: create or present a problem, question, or threat

ex.en: Climate change poses a serious threat to the planet.
ex.ko: 기후 변화는 지구에 심각한 위협을 제기한다.

pose.en: (pose as) pretend to be someone else in order to deceive people

ex.en: The thief posed as a police officer to enter the house.
ex.ko: 그 도둑은 경찰인 척하며 집에 들어갔다.

posh.en: (informal, especially British) expensive, fashionable, and associated with the upper class

ex.en: They stayed at a posh hotel in London.
ex.ko: 그들은 런던의 고급스러운 호텔에 묵었다.

possessed.en: completely controlled by a strong feeling or desire

ex.en: He acted like a man possessed, working day and night.
ex.ko: 그는 밤낮으로 일하며 마치 무언가에 홀린 사람처럼 행동했다.

ex.en: In the movie, the house was possessed by a ghost.
ex.ko: 그 영화에서 그 집은 귀신에 들려 있었다.

postal.en: relating to the system of collecting and delivering mail

ex.en: Please check the postal code before sending the letter.
ex.ko: 편지를 보내기 전에 우편번호를 확인하세요.

postpone.en: change an event to a later time or date

ex.en: The meeting was postponed until next week.
ex.ko: 회의는 다음 주로 연기되었다.

potent.en: having a strong effect or influence

ex.en: The medicine is a potent painkiller.
ex.ko: 그 약은 효능이 강한 진통제다.

potent.en: very convincing or effective, especially in an argument

ex.en: She made a potent argument for the new policy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 정책에 대해 설득력 있는 주장을 펼쳤다.

potentially.en: possibly, but not yet certainly or actually

ex.en: The chemical is potentially dangerous if inhaled.
ex.ko: 그 화학 물질은 흡입하면 잠재적으로 위험할 수 있다.

powerfully.en: in a way that has great strength, force, or effect

ex.en: The speech powerfully moved the entire audience.
ex.ko: 그 연설은 청중 전체를 강력하게 감동시켰다.

practicality.en: the quality of being useful or sensible in a real situation

ex.en: We need to consider the practicality of this plan.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 계획의 실용성을 고려해야 한다.

ex.en: The project is practically finished.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 거의 다 끝났다.

practically.en: in a sensible and realistic way

ex.en: We need to approach this problem practically.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 문제에 실용적으로 접근해야 한다.

prance.en: walk or move in a proud, lively way, often to attract attention

ex.en: The model pranced down the runway.
ex.ko: 그 모델은 런웨이를 뽐내듯 걸었다.

prawn.en: a small edible sea creature with a shell, similar to a shrimp but usually larger

ex.en: We ordered grilled prawns for dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁으로 구운 새우를 주문했다.

precede.en: happen or exist before something else in time or order

ex.en: A loud noise preceded the explosion.
ex.ko: 큰 소리가 폭발보다 먼저 있었다.

precisely.en: exactly, with no error or variation

ex.en: The train arrived at precisely 9 a.m.
ex.ko: 기차는 정확히 오전 9시에 도착했다.

predicament.en: a difficult or unpleasant situation that is hard to escape

ex.en: Losing his passport put him in a difficult predicament.
ex.ko: 여권을 잃어버려서 그는 곤란한 상황에 처했다.

predicate.en: (grammar) the part of a sentence that says something about the subject, usually containing the verb

ex.en: In the sentence "She sings beautifully," the predicate is "sings beautifully."
ex.ko: "She sings beautifully"라는 문장에서 서술어는 "sings beautifully"이다.

predicate.en: (predicated on) base something on a particular fact or belief

ex.en: The plan is predicated on strong sales growth.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 강한 매출 성장에 근거하고 있다.

predicative.en: (grammar) describing an adjective used after a verb like 'be', rather than directly before a noun

ex.en: In "The soup is hot," 'hot' is used predicatively.
ex.ko: "The soup is hot"에서 'hot'은 서술적으로 쓰였다.

predictable.en: able to be known or guessed in advance

ex.en: The ending of the movie was very predictable.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 결말은 매우 예측 가능했다.

ex.en: The software uses predictive text to suggest words.
ex.ko: 그 소프트웨어는 단어를 제안하는 데 예측 텍스트 기능을 사용한다.

predictor.en: a person who predicts future events

ex.en: Weather predictors are not always accurate.
ex.ko: 날씨를 예측하는 사람들이 항상 정확한 것은 아니다.

predictor.en: a factor or piece of information that helps forecast a future outcome

ex.en: Education level is a strong predictor of income.
ex.ko: 교육 수준은 소득을 예측하는 강력한 요인이다.

prefect.en: a chief official or magistrate in certain countries or historical periods

ex.en: The prefect was responsible for governing the province.
ex.ko: 그 지사는 그 지방을 다스릴 책임이 있었다.

prefect.en: (in British schools) an older student given some authority to help supervise younger students

ex.en: As a prefect, she helped keep order in the hallways.
ex.ko: 학생회 임원으로서 그녀는 복도 질서를 유지하는 것을 도왔다.

preferable.en: more desirable or suitable than another option

ex.en: A quiet evening at home is preferable to a crowded party.
ex.ko: 붐비는 파티보다 집에서 보내는 조용한 저녁이 더 낫다.

preferably.en: used to say what you would like to happen if it is possible

ex.en: Send the report by email, preferably before noon.
ex.ko: 가급적 정오 전까지 이메일로 보고서를 보내주세요.

prehistoric.en: belonging to the time before written history began

ex.en: They found prehistoric cave paintings.
ex.ko: 그들은 선사시대 동굴 벽화를 발견했다.

ex.en: This computer is practically prehistoric.
ex.ko: 이 컴퓨터는 거의 구닥다리 수준이다.

prehuman.en: existing or happening before humans appeared on Earth

ex.en: Scientists study prehuman species to understand evolution.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 진화를 이해하기 위해 인류 이전 종들을 연구한다.

preindustrial.en: relating to the time before industry and factories became widespread

ex.en: Preindustrial societies relied mainly on farming.
ex.ko: 산업화 이전 사회는 주로 농업에 의존했다.

prejudge.en: judge someone or something before having enough information or evidence

ex.en: Try not to prejudge the situation before hearing both sides.
ex.ko: 양쪽 이야기를 듣기 전에 상황을 예단하지 않도록 해라.

preliminary.en: happening before or in preparation for something more important

ex.en: We conducted a preliminary study before the main experiment.
ex.ko: 우리는 본 실험 전에 예비 연구를 진행했다.

preliminary.en: an event that happens before and prepares for a more important one

ex.en: The preliminaries will determine who advances to the finals.
ex.ko: 예선에서 누가 결승에 진출할지 결정된다.

preoccupy.en: fill someone's mind so much that they cannot think about anything else

ex.en: Worries about the exam preoccupied her all week.
ex.ko: 시험에 대한 걱정이 일주일 내내 그녀의 머릿속을 채웠다.

prescribe.en: (of a doctor) officially recommend the use of a medicine or treatment

ex.en: The doctor prescribed antibiotics for the infection.
ex.ko: 의사는 감염 치료를 위해 항생제를 처방했다.

prescribe.en: (formal) state officially that something must be done or is required

ex.en: The law prescribes strict penalties for fraud.
ex.ko: 그 법은 사기에 대해 엄격한 처벌을 규정하고 있다.

presenter.en: a person who gives a talk or presentation to an audience

ex.en: The presenter explained the new marketing strategy.
ex.ko: 발표자는 새로운 마케팅 전략을 설명했다.

presenter.en: a person who introduces and hosts a television or radio program

ex.en: She has been a news presenter for ten years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 10년째 뉴스 진행자로 일하고 있다.

presidency.en: the position or period of time of being president

ex.en: He served two terms as president during his presidency.
ex.ko: 그는 대통령 재임 중 두 번의 임기를 지냈다.

presidential.en: relating to a president or the office of president

ex.en: The presidential election will take place in November.
ex.ko: 대통령 선거는 11월에 치러질 것이다.

prestige.en: a high level of respect that comes from success, influence, or wealth

ex.en: The university has great academic prestige.
ex.ko: 그 대학은 뛰어난 학문적 명성을 지니고 있다.

prestigious.en: respected and admired as very important or of high quality

ex.en: She won a prestigious award for her research.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 연구로 권위 있는 상을 받았다.

presumably.en: used to say that you think something is probably true, based on the information you have

ex.en: Presumably, he forgot about the meeting.
ex.ko: 아마도 그는 회의를 잊어버린 것 같다.

prevail.en: prove more powerful or successful than opposition

ex.en: Justice prevailed in the end.
ex.ko: 결국 정의가 승리했다.

prevail.en: exist widely or be the most common situation at a particular time

ex.en: Calm weather prevailed throughout the weekend.
ex.ko: 주말 내내 잔잔한 날씨가 이어졌다.

prevention.en: the act of stopping something bad from happening

ex.en: Prevention is better than cure.
ex.ko: 예방이 치료보다 낫다.

preventive.en: intended to stop something bad from happening, especially illness

ex.en: Regular exercise is a good preventive measure against heart disease.
ex.ko: 규칙적인 운동은 심장병을 막는 좋은 예방책이다.

previously.en: at an earlier time; before now or before a particular time

ex.en: She had previously worked as a teacher.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이전에 교사로 일했었다.

priceless.cue: 돈으로 값을 매길 수 없을 만큼 귀한
priceless.ko: 값을 매길 수 없는, 매우 귀중한
priceless.en: so valuable that its worth cannot be measured in money

ex.en: The museum displays priceless works of art.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 값을 매길 수 없는 귀중한 예술 작품들을 전시한다.

prick.en: make a small hole in something with a sharp point, such as a needle

ex.en: She pricked her finger while sewing.
ex.ko: 그녀는 바느질을 하다가 손가락을 찔렸다.

prick.en: a sharp, small pain caused by something pointed

ex.en: He felt a sudden prick on his arm.
ex.ko: 그는 팔에 갑자기 따끔한 통증을 느꼈다.

ex.en: Her primary concern is her children's safety.
ex.ko: 그녀의 가장 큰 관심사는 아이들의 안전이다.

primary.en: basic, or being the first stage

ex.en: Reading and writing are taught in primary school.
ex.ko: 읽기와 쓰기는 초등학교에서 가르친다.

primary.en: (US) an election in which voters choose a candidate to represent a political party

ex.en: He won the Democratic primary in his state.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 주에서 민주당 예비선거에 승리했다.

prime.en: main, most important, or of the best quality

ex.en: This is a prime example of good design.
ex.ko: 이것은 훌륭한 디자인의 주된 예시다.

prime.en: (mathematics) a number that can only be divided evenly by 1 and itself

ex.en: Seven is a prime number.
ex.ko: 7은 소수이다.

prime.en: the period when someone is at their strongest, most successful, or most attractive

ex.en: The athlete is now in the prime of his career.
ex.ko: 그 선수는 지금 커리어의 전성기를 맞고 있다.

prime minister.en: the leader of the government in many countries

ex.en: The Prime Minister announced new economic reforms.
ex.ko: 총리는 새로운 경제 개혁을 발표했다.

prior.en: existing or happening before something else, or before a particular time

ex.en: She has no prior experience in this field.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이 분야에서 사전 경험이 없다.

priority.cue: 가장 먼저 처리해야 할 중요한 것
priority.en: something that is more important than other things and should be dealt with first

ex.en: Safety is our top priority.
ex.ko: 안전이 우리의 최우선 순위다.

privately.en: without other people knowing or being present; not publicly

ex.en: She told me privately that she was leaving the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나에게 회사를 떠날 것이라고 개인적으로 말했다.

privately.en: by a private person, company, or organization rather than the government

ex.en: The hospital is privately funded.
ex.ko: 그 병원은 민간이 자금을 지원한다.

privilege.en: a special right or advantage that only some people have

ex.en: Education is a privilege many people take for granted.
ex.ko: 교육은 많은 사람들이 당연하게 여기는 특권이다.

probable.en: likely to happen or be true, though not certain

ex.en: A recession seems probable this year.
ex.ko: 올해는 경기 침체가 있을 가능성이 커 보인다.

probe.en: ask questions or examine something closely to find out the truth

ex.en: Reporters probed the politician about the scandal.
ex.ko: 기자들은 그 정치인에게 스캔들에 대해 캐물었다.

ex.en: The police launched a probe into the company's finances.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 회사의 재정에 대한 조사에 착수했다.

probe.en: an unmanned spacecraft sent to explore and gather information about space

ex.en: The space probe sent images back from Mars.
ex.ko: 그 우주 탐사선은 화성에서 이미지를 전송했다.

procedure.en: a series of actions done in a particular order to complete a task

ex.en: Please follow the correct procedure for submitting your application.
ex.ko: 지원서 제출을 위한 올바른 절차를 따라주세요.

ex.en: The surgical procedure took about two hours.
ex.ko: 그 수술은 약 두 시간이 걸렸다.

profile.en: the outline of a person's face when seen from the side

ex.en: She has a striking profile.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인상적인 옆모습을 가지고 있다.

profile.en: a short description of a person's or organization's background and characteristics

ex.en: Fill out your profile on the app before you start.
ex.ko: 시작하기 전에 앱에 프로필을 작성하세요.

profile.en: (keep/have a low/high profile) the level of public attention someone or something receives

ex.en: The company has kept a low profile since the scandal.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 스캔들 이후 주목받지 않으려 조용히 지내고 있다.

profit.en: money that is gained in business after costs have been paid

ex.en: The company made a huge profit last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 큰 수익을 냈다.

profit.en: gain an advantage or benefit from something

ex.en: You can profit from learning a new skill.
ex.ko: 새로운 기술을 배우면 득을 볼 수 있다.

profitable.en: producing a profit or financial gain

ex.en: The new business quickly became profitable.
ex.ko: 그 신규 사업은 빠르게 수익성 있게 되었다.

programmer.en: a person who writes and tests computer programs

ex.en: She works as a software programmer at a tech company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기술 회사에서 소프트웨어 프로그래머로 일한다.
